introSection: Mystic Timbers is wooden roller coaster located at Kings Island in Mason, Ohio, USA. It was manufactured by Great Coasters International, designed by Skyline Attractions, and has controls provided by Irvine Ondrey Engineering. The ride opened on April 15, 2017.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Mystic Timbers was announced on July 28, 2016. Vertical construction began in August 2016. In November 2016, the first drop was completed. While the layout of the ride was revealed, the final enclosed element was kept as a secret until the ride's opening day on April 15, 2017.

On September 9, 2017, Mystic Timbers was voted the Best New Ride of 2017 in the Amusement Today Golden Ticket Awards.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Mystic Timbers has a similar first and second drop to Prowler at Worlds of Fun.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content:
Mystic Timbers is located in the Rivertown-themed area of Kings Island. Just before the ride ends, guests go into a themed building. The theatrical element was also hinted at by Kings Island's public relations team, who used the hashtag "WhatsInTheShed" to market the ride. The shed was later revealed to be a special effects show, with visuals provided by Holovis.

There is a single tunnel along the course of the ride.

The ride includes a custom score by IMAscore GbR.

fullText:
Mystic Timbers is wooden roller coaster located at Kings Island in Mason, Ohio, USA. It was manufactured by Great Coasters International, designed by Skyline Attractions, and has controls provided by Irvine Ondrey Engineering. The ride opened on April 15, 2017.

Mystic Timbers was announced on July 28, 2016. Vertical construction began in August 2016. In November 2016, the first drop was completed. While the layout of the ride was revealed, the final enclosed element was kept as a secret until the ride's opening day on April 15, 2017.

On September 9, 2017, Mystic Timbers was voted the Best New Ride of 2017 in the Amusement Today Golden Ticket Awards.

Mystic Timbers has a similar first and second drop to Prowler at Worlds of Fun.



Mystic Timbers is located in the Rivertown-themed area of Kings Island. Just before the ride ends, guests go into a themed building. The theatrical element was also hinted at by Kings Island's public relations team, who used the hashtag "WhatsInTheShed" to market the ride. The shed was later revealed to be a special effects show, with visuals provided by Holovis.

There is a single tunnel along the course of the ride.

The ride includes a custom score by IMAscore GbR.
